message:
feat(frankenterm-core): add wa-mcp-envelope JSON schema for MCP tool responses

Adds a dedicated JSON-schema golden for the standard envelope returned
inside MCP tool text payloads, alongside the existing wa-robot-envelope
schema for the robot HTTP API. Both envelopes share the ok / error /
elapsed_ms / version / now shape but the MCP envelope additionally
requires `mcp_version` and uses a distinct FT-MCP-NNNN error-code
namespace, so it gets its own goldens + conformance suite.

New schema:

- docs/json-schema/wa-mcp-envelope.json (92 lines, draft-2020-12)
  describes McpEnvelope<T>: required {ok, elapsed_ms, version, now,
  mcp_version} with optional data on success and {error, error_code,
  hint} on failure. error_code is constrained via $ref to the
  FT-MCP-0001 / FT-MCP-0002 / … namespace defined under $defs.
  additionalProperties=false to keep the wire form locked.

Provenance:

- docs/json-schema/PROVENANCE.md gains a row mapping the schema to its
  Rust source-of-truth — McpEnvelope<T> in frankenterm-core/mcp_types.rs
  and the MCP_ERR_* constants in frankenterm-core/mcp_error.rs — with
  the same "verify with cargo test … --test schema_golden /
  conformance_robot_envelope_schema" recipe used for the robot
  envelope.

Test wiring:

- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/schema_golden.rs:
  * schema_files_have_required_fields and registry_covers_all_disk_schemas
    add wa-mcp-envelope.json to the existing wa-robot-envelope.json /
    ft-config.json exemption sets — these are response-shape /
    configuration goldens, not endpoint-output goldens, so they belong
    outside the endpoint registry.
  * Stale rustfmt drift on the api_schema/docs_gen import block is
    absorbed in the same hunk (mechanical reorder, no symbol changes).

- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/conformance_robot_envelope_schema.rs:
  * Renames envelope_schema_path → robot_envelope_schema_path and adds
    a sibling mcp_envelope_schema_path so each schema has a clearly
    named loader.
  * Factors out a generic load_schema(&Path) helper so
    load_envelope_schema (robot) and load_mcp_envelope_schema (MCP)
    share validation setup.
  * Adds synthetic_robot_error_envelope_with_hint_validates plus
    matching MCP-side fixtures so the new schema is exercised end-to-
    end against the same draft-2020-12 validator.
  * Drops one rustfmt drift (panic block reformat) along the way.

No production-runtime code changes; the new schema is consumed by tests
and the `ft-mcp` documentation tooling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ The `Last regenerated version` column is the workspace package version from
 | `wa-robot-agents-list.json` | `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs::RobotAgentsListData` and `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs::AgentInventoryData` | Manual golden update from Rust serde structs; verify with `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test schema_golden` | 0.1.0 |
 | `wa-robot-agents-running.json` | `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs::RobotAgentsRunningData` and `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs::AgentInventoryData` | Manual golden update from Rust serde structs; verify with `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test schema_golden` | 0.1.0 |
 | `wa-robot-approve.json` | `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs::RobotApproveData`; client mirror `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs::ApproveData` | Manual golden update from Rust serde structs; verify with `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test schema_golden` | 0.1.0 |
+| `wa-mcp-envelope.json` | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs::McpEnvelope<T>` and `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_error.rs::MCP_ERR_*` | Manual golden update from Rust serde structs and MCP error constants; verify with `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test conformance_robot_envelope_schema` and `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test schema_golden` | 0.1.0 |
 | `wa-robot-envelope.json` | `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs::RobotResponse<T>`; client mirror `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs::RobotResponse<T>` | Manual golden update from Rust serde structs; verify with `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test conformance_robot_envelope_schema` and `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test schema_golden` | 0.1.0 |
 | `wa-robot-event-mutation.json` | `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs::RobotEventMutationData`; client mirror `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs::EventMutationData` | Manual golden update from Rust serde structs; verify with `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test schema_golden` | 0.1.0 |
 | `wa-robot-events.json` | `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs::RobotEventsData`; client mirror `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs::EventsData` | Manual golden update from Rust serde structs; verify with `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test schema_golden` | 0.1.0 |

@@ -0,0 +1,92 @@
+{
+  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
+  "$id": "https://wezterm-automata.dev/schemas/wa-mcp-envelope.json",
+  "title": "WA MCP Tool Response Envelope",
+  "description": "Standard JSON envelope for FrankenTerm MCP tool responses. MCP tools return this structure inside the tool text payload with tool-specific data in the 'data' field on success and FT-MCP error codes on failure.",
+  "type": "object",
+  "required": ["ok", "elapsed_ms", "version", "now", "mcp_version"],
+  "additionalProperties": false,
+  "properties": {
+    "ok": {
+      "type": "boolean",
+      "description": "Whether the MCP tool call succeeded. When true, 'data' contains the result. When false, 'error' and 'error_code' describe the failure."
+    },
+    "data": {
+      "description": "Tool-specific response data. Present when ok=true. Type depends on the specific MCP tool."
+    },
+    "error": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "description": "Human-readable error message. Present when ok=false."
+    },
+    "error_code": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "description": "Machine-readable MCP error code for programmatic handling. Present when ok=false. Format: 'FT-MCP-0001'.",
+      "$ref": "#/$defs/error_codes"
+    },
+    "hint": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "description": "Actionable suggestion for resolving the error. Present when ok=false and a remediation is known."
+    },
+    "elapsed_ms": {
+      "type": "integer",
+      "minimum": 0,
+      "description": "Time taken to execute the tool call in milliseconds."
+    },
+    "version": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "description": "frankenterm version string (semver format).",
+      "pattern": "^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+.*$"
+    },
+    "now": {
+      "type": "integer",
+      "minimum": 0,
+      "description": "Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the response was generated."
+    },
+    "mcp_version": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "description": "MCP envelope version emitted by the runtime.",
+      "const": "v1"
+    }
+  },
+  "if": {
+    "properties": { "ok": { "const": true } }
+  },
+  "then": {
+    "required": ["data"],
+    "not": {
+      "anyOf": [
+        { "required": ["error"] },
+        { "required": ["error_code"] },
+        { "required": ["hint"] }
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "else": {
+    "required": ["error", "error_code"],
+    "not": {
+      "required": ["data"]
+    }
+  },
+  "$defs": {
+    "error_codes": {
+      "title": "Standard MCP Tool Error Codes",
+      "description": "Well-known FT-MCP error codes returned by MCP tool envelopes",
+      "enum": [
+        "FT-MCP-0001",
+        "FT-MCP-0003",
+        "FT-MCP-0004",
+        "FT-MCP-0005",
+        "FT-MCP-0006",
+        "FT-MCP-0007",
+        "FT-MCP-0008",
+        "FT-MCP-0009",
+        "FT-MCP-0010",
+        "FT-MCP-0011",
+        "FT-MCP-0012",
+        "FT-MCP-0013",
+        "FT-MCP-0014",
+        "FT-MCP-0015"
+      ]
+    }
+  }
+}
